vulnerability.title: Implement STIG Sub Crypto Policy
vulnerability.description: Create a custom cryptographic policy to follow the guidance from DISA.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: cat << 'EOF' > /etc/crypto-policies/policies/modules/STIG.pmod cipher@SSH=AES-256-GCM AES-256-CTR AES-128-GCM AES-128-CTR mac@SSH=HMAC-SHA2-512 HMAC-SHA2-256 EOF sudo update-crypto-policies --set FIPS:STIG

triage_decision.reason: The remediation creates a custom crypto policy using standard, strong ciphers and MACs and applies it via update-crypto-policies. This is a typical STIG hardening step for SSH and system crypto and is unlikely to lock out access or break core services, though there is a minor risk of incompatibility with legacy clients or non‑FIPS‑compliant applications.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Potential incompatibility with older SSH clients; Possible breakage of non‑FIPS‑compliant applications
